title: Explain Memory
short_answer: Memory keeps useful information so the assistant can use it later.
text: In this app, memory is not just storage. It is a system that prepares, stores, searches, and returns useful context when a task needs it.

title: Explain Context Memory
short_answer: Context Memory is the engine that turns raw activity into usable context.
text: Context Memory reads inputs, organizes useful facts, builds snapshots, stores memory, and retrieves the right information for a task. It is the app's memory and context engine.

title: Explain Context Memory Folder
short_answer: app/context_memory is the main memory and context engine folder.
text: The context_memory folder owns the logic for ingest, normalize, classify, extract, build, store, retrieve, summarize, and serve memory/context to the rest of the app.

title: Explain Ingest
short_answer: Ingest reads raw sources and turns them into memory inputs.
text: Ingest is where new input sources should enter the memory system. Examples are chat history, session events, screenshots, files, and runtime state.

title: Explain Classify
short_answer: Classify decides what an input means and how important it is.
text: The classify layer contains product logic. It helps decide memory kind, source type, importance, recency, promotion, and routing filters.

title: Explain Retrieval
short_answer: Retrieval searches memory according to the current task.
text: Retrieval is not just finding text. It should return the right memory for the task, such as code context, timeline lookup, project follow-up, or user preference retrieval.

title: Explain Backends
short_answer: Backends are replaceable storage and index layers.
text: Backends let the memory architecture keep its logic while changing storage. JSON, Mem0, and Qdrant can be used as different backend layers.

title: Explain Mem0
short_answer: Mem0 is one possible memory backend, not the whole architecture.
text: Mem0 can be used as an integration layer inside the memory system. The app should not depend directly on Mem0 because it is only one replaceable backend.

title: Explain Qdrant
short_answer: Qdrant supports semantic-style search and ranking for memory.
text: Qdrant is useful when exact word search is not enough. In this app, it should help retrieval and indexing, but it should not replace the memory architecture by itself.

title: Explain Services
short_answer: Services are the public access layer for the memory block.
text: Other app blocks should call memory through services instead of reading internal files directly. The main idea is to keep memory clean, stable, and replaceable.
